Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: We all love a quilt that looks harder than it is! Join Angela for a nine-patch within a nine-patch within, yes, another nine-patch.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Hold on to your sewing chairs! The Midnight Quilt Show is going bold, as in late-night foundation paper-piecing bold. (Angela makes it easy!)
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: We're having trouble seeing straight. Then again, it's probably the Drunkard's Path block! Angela's here to make sure the steps go "straight to your head."
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Today we take a very personal approach using our names and monograms. Our first projects are monogram ornaments made from air dry clay. Then make initial letter necklaces for you and your friends. Today's art journal technique is lettering -- learn some tips for simple calligraphy. Finally make an initial bulletin board covered with initials and doodle art.
Video.title: Lettering

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: George Vondriska shows you how to make an occasional table to provide a convenient place for drinks, TV remotes, or a decorative lamp. He demonstrates the unique process of ebonizing, an alternative to stain used to give wood a rich, dark finish. With attractive tapered legs and a ceramic tabletop, this table is a great project to advance your woodworking skills.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Occasional Table

Video.summary: Artists are known for taking an unconventional approach to their art – often introducing "non art" supplies to their work. First we look at stamped metal for inventive beads. Then Candie Cooper throws a curve and uses cookie tins for her project. Next Kate Richbourg throws out the loom for a lalique bracelet that is loom free. We'll finish up with artist Tammy Honaman as she manipulates right angle weave to turn it into shapes and beads.
Video.title: Unconventional Art

Video.summary: Good things come in small packages. Create a mini book from a 3D kaleidoscope with Joe Rotella, a mini journal with Jenny Barnett Rohrs, and 3D sculptured surface for your journal with Kim Hupke. All today on Scrapbook Soup.
Video.title: Mini Books

Video.summary: Making a dresser is a milestone project for many woodworkers. George Vondriska shares some essential skills that you'll need to craft a quality dresser that blends form and function. He'll dive into dimensions, construction, materials, drawers, and more. Then, follow along with his in-depth demonstration as he cuts and assembles the pieces of his dresser.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Dresser Essentials

Video.summary: Explore your inner child and let them out during playtime on this episode of Fresh Quilting. Steph Skardal encourages playing with color starting with paper before even adding fabric. Then, Catherine Cureton plays around with a mini quilt to turn it into a functional pillow. Eliane Bergmann has a machine tip on presser feet - when do you raise and when do you lower – what is that knob on your machine?
Video.title: Playtime

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: THANK YOU to all of you amazing quilters for supporting this journey and show! Let's take a look back at 2017 and forward to 2018!
Video.title: Year In Review

Video.summary: Angela brings you her laurel wreath quilt, the perfect excuse to showcase your most prized scraps and highlight it all with gorgeous free-motion quilting.
Video.title: Laurel Wreath

Video.summary: Ever started a gift quilt that ended up being too good to give away? You're not the only one. Angela's second quilt ever was supposed to be for her mom, then… well, let's just say she got sidetracked. But she's making up for it with a new episode that's just for, you guessed it, Mom.
Video.title: Blooming Medallion

Video.summary: In this season finale, Angela does a little flower arranging (quilter's style!) to create Monique Jacobs' beautiful Bouquet quilt.
Video.title: Flower Arranging

Video.summary: On the season finale of Season 6 of the Midnight Quilt Show, follow along as Angela shares her clever take on a braided quilt pattern she created herself!
Video.title: Braided Star Quilt

Video.summary: This is not a drill! Angela has heard your requests loud and clear, and she's finally making the Bargello quilt that everyone's been asking for! So break out the layer cakes and prepare to get crazy with precuts.
Video.title: Modern Bargello

Video.summary: The holidays are coming, but don't stress! We teamed up with Angela Walters, host of the Midnight Quilt Show, to create three DIY quilt projects that are super gift-able. In this episode, you'll learn how to make a cathedral window pillow, an adorable gift for someone in your life who you've deemed "quilt-worthy." - P.S. Don't miss Angela's tips for non-quilters on what to do (and what NOT to do!) when you receive a gift from a quilter...
Video.title: Cathedral Window Pillow

Video.summary: Angela is kicking off Season 7 of the Midnight Quilt Show with a redemption story. She’s made this Thistle quilt pattern before with half-square triangles but thinks this flying-geese version will be much easier to quilt.
Video.title: Flying Geese Quilt Redo!

Video.summary: Hollywood designers and glamorous starlets set the trends as people flocked to the movies in the 1930's. Rebecca Kemp-Brent shows how to recreate any neckline for a whole new look. Angela Wolf embroiders fabric to create a gown for the red carpet. Joanne Banko shows how to join lace, ribbon and fabric with the edge joining foot to get a designer look.
Video.title: The Glamourous - 1930's

Video.summary: Every once in a while, your sewing project needs a little more. Add ons can add that extra dose of style that makes your sewing unique. First, Emily Thompson adds embroidered cuffs to her adorable PJ's. Then, Sarah Gunn takes a cute skirt and makes it amazing with ribbon. Finally, hear about Angela Wolf's sewing journey in our designer spotlight
Video.title: Add Ons

Video.summary: Wire wrapping is one of our all time favorite techniques; keep it fresh with these updates on tried and true techniques. Make a layered pendant necklace, learn how to wire wrap bangle bracelets using sliders with Barb Switzer, last, make earrings with Patti Bullard. Wire it, wear it and love it.
Video.title: Wire It

Video.summary: Bend it, twist it, shape it, wear it. Jewelry designer Sandra Lupo warms up by bending and shaping sheet metal with a tool she invented. Then, she enhances the designs with patterns, texture and color. Host and designer Katie Hacker make a quick and easy charm bracelet to cool down.
Video.title: Shape Up

Video.summary: Let's liven things up! Joe Rotella makes a delightful owl clock. Cheryl Boglioli saves time as she creates colorful backgrounds for journals and scrapbooks. May Flaum has a great tip for adding interest to her pages. Christine Drumheller stays on schedule with a daily planner personalized with stickers and laminated pages.
Video.title: Episode 5: Time

Video.summary: Living creatures are all around us in nature. Host Julie Fei Fan Balzer begins with paper beetles; a fun and easy project drawing project. Then Cheryl Holz has a dragonfly portrait made with transparent overlays. Next Cat Kerr has a wall hanging made from an embroidery hoop featuring pigs. We finish with a nature inspired doodling meditation from Tori Weyers.
Video.title: Episode 6: Living Creatures

Video.summary: Get out your rose-colored glasses, or whatever props you need to take a fresh look at your art. Host Julie Fei Fan Balzer starts the show with a faux shibori dyeing demonstration. Then, our tool guy, Joe Rotella creates unique frames using small strips of wood for a rustic or metallic look. Finally, Tori Weyers returns this season for more artistic inspiration. Every other week she has a lesson on a new way of looking at an aspect of art through your sketchbook. Today it's all about words. Explore interesting textures and shapes through writing and letters.
Video.title: Episode 1: Fresh Looks

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Hold onto your needles! Tracie and Jodi, are here to dish out fresh fiber inspiration. From project picks to wooly good gossip, things are about to get super knitty… and maybe just a little nutty.
Video.title: Episode 12

Video.summary: Forget wool and alpaca fiber; upcycled socks and grocery bags are where it's at! Tune in to find out how to turn any tubular (and cuttable) material into knittable yarn.
Video.title: UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!

Image.alt: UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!
UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!
UPCYCLED Knitting: Turn Denim, Cotton & Plastic into Yarn!

Video.summary: As if you needed more reasons to knit... The Grocery Girls are back with the 11 reasons you should be knitting every day, and some of them might surprise you!
Video.title: 11 Reasons You Should Knit Every Day

Image.alt: 11 Reasons You Should Knit Every Day
11 Reasons You Should Knit Every Day
11 Reasons You Should Knit Every Day

Video.summary: Having trouble with your Fox Paws scarf? Well, you're not the only one! Luckily, designer Xandy is here to share her tips and tricks for navigating her famously intricate pattern.
Video.title: Stacked Increases + FOX PAWS Knitting Pattern

Image.alt: Stacked Increases + FOX PAWS Knitting Pattern
Stacked Increases + FOX PAWS Knitting Pattern
Stacked Increases + FOX PAWS Knitting Pattern

Video.summary: Let's turn toward ourselves for creative ideas. When she makes a lighted mirror, Jenny Barnett-Rohrs makes you a star. Katie Hacker makes personal paper dolls and Franz Spohn draws a 3D self-portrait. Jenny celebrates Thanksgiving with Pilgrim-style table decorations.
Video.title: Me!

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Artist's Academy presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
